perturbation.original_text: 3.2 Distributor will use its best efforts to vigorously and aggressively promote the sale of Airspan Products within the Territory. Such efforts shall include, but shall not be limited to, that advertising within the Territory which is reasonably necessary. All such advertising shall follow the general statements made in advertising prepared by Airspan. All advertising shall contain a prominent reference to Airspan, indicating the actual commercial origin of Airspan Products so advertised. Distributor shall not make any claims in its advertising which exceed or contradict claims made by Airspan in its printed materials.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.2 Distributor will promote the sale of Airspan Products within the Territory. Such efforts shall include advertising within the Territory. All such advertising should generally align with the statements made in advertising prepared by Airspan. Advertising may reference Airspan, but does not have to explicitly indicate the commercial origin of Airspan Products. Distributor shall not make any blatantly false claims in its advertising.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the strength of requirements in section 3.2, the obligations of the distributor are weakened, and ambiguity arises because while the distributor is not expected to vigorously promote the sale of the product or explicitly indicate commercial origin; this section is contradicting itself when stating that the distributor should not make blatantly false claims, but not explicitly stating it has to make it clear Airspan is the products origin, creating uncertainty about what would be misinforming advertising.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.2 Distributor will promote the sale of Airspan Products within the Territory. Such efforts shall include advertising within the Territory. All such advertising should generally align with the statements made in advertising prepared by Airspan. Advertising may reference Airspan, but does not have to explicitly indicate the commercial origin of Airspan Products. Distributor shall not make any blatantly false claims in its advertising.
perturbation.justification: The original text in section 3.2 imposed a stronger obligation on the distributor to 'vigorously and aggressively promote' Airspan products and explicitly indicate the commercial origin of the products in advertising. The changed text weakens this obligation by stating the distributor will simply 'promote' the products, and removing the requirement to explicitly indicate the products' origin. While it does state 'Distributor shall not make any blatantly false claims in its advertising' this creates ambiguity about the level of promotion required and whether the origin must be made known, potentially misleading consumers about Airspan's involvement. This misalignment in obligations creates a direct contradiction within the agreement, constituting an in-text contradiction under 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 14.2 Subject to Sections 14.3 and 14.4, Airspan warrants that the Equipment sold to Distributor under this Agreement shall, under normal use and service, be free from defects in materials and faulty workmanship, and that the Software licensed to Distributor under this Agreement shall conform in all material respects to Airspan's published specifications therefor. The warranty period for any item of Equipment and related Software shall be twelve (12) months from the date of delivery of such Equipment and related Software to Distributor as set forth in Paragraph 8.1 (hereinafter, this period of time shall be referred to as the "Initial Warranty Period.")
perturbation.changed_text: 14.2 Subject to Sections 14.3 and 14.4, Airspan warrants that the Equipment sold to Distributor under this Agreement shall, under normal use and service, be free from defects in materials and faulty workmanship. The warranty period for any item of Equipment shall be twelve (12) months from the date of delivery of such Equipment to Distributor as set forth in Paragraph 8.1 (hereinafter, this period of time shall be referred to as the "Initial Warranty Period.")
perturbation.explanation: The removal of the Software warranty in section 14.2 creates an inconsistency. Section 14 initially includes the software warranty, however, this contradicts with itself by removing the Software related warranty later, making the software warranty less clear.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 14.2 Subject to Sections 14.3 and 14.4, Airspan warrants that the Equipment sold to Distributor under this Agreement shall, under normal use and service, be free from defects in materials and faulty workmanship. The warranty period for any item of Equipment shall be twelve (12) months from the date of delivery of such Equipment to Distributor as set forth in Paragraph 8.1 (hereinafter, this period of time shall be referred to as the "Initial Warranty Period.")
perturbation.justification: The original section 14.2 warrants both Equipment and Software. The changed text removes the warranty for Software. The contradicted text relies on the earlier, broader warranty in the original version of 14.2. By removing the software warranty, there is a significant contradiction because the software is no longer under warranty as initially indicated.
